hyperedges.label: B2B Commercialization Core: Org Isolation + Role Enforcement + Billing
Cron Alert Delivery Pipeline: GitHub Action → API Endpoint → Notifications
RLS Multi-Tenancy Isolation Group: RLS Policies + current_org_ids() + org_memberships

nodes.label: investment_categories (Global Shared Table - Data Leak Risk)
nodes.rationale: investment_categories is not scoped to any tenant (no user_id, no org_id). This is a data-isolation bug disguised as a feature — the day a second tenant signs up, both see/edit the same rows. Must be migrated to per-org in Step 1 of commercialization.
nodes.norm_label: investment_categories (global shared table - data leak risk)

nodes.label: Cron Alert Layer (GitHub Action + /api/cron/alerts + Gmail SMTP)
nodes.norm_label: cron alert layer (github action + /api/cron/alerts + gmail smtp)

nodes.label: audit_log Table (No RLS, Appears Unwritten)
nodes.rationale: audit_log table exists but has no RLS policies and no evidence it is being written to in any server actions. For a B2B finance product, an enforced tenant-scoped audit log is a compliance selling feature, not an afterthought. Should be wired in Step 1 RLS sweep.

nodes.label: Model A — Shared Multi-Tenant SaaS (Recommended)
nodes.rationale: Recommended as starting point due to lowest marginal cost per customer. Schema work (org_id isolation) is unavoidable for any serious B2B play regardless of model chosen. Isolation shifts from user_id to org_id via RLS.

nodes.label: Model B — White-Label Dedicated Deployment
nodes.rationale: Each customer gets their own Supabase project + Netlify site. Operational cost grows linearly with customer count. Suited for regulated enterprise clients (banks, government finance offices) needing data sovereignty. Deferred until a paying customer demands it.

nodes.label: Model C — Hybrid (Shared Core + Dedicated Tier)
nodes.rationale: Build Model A first, architect so exact same codebase can be deployed as single-tenant for enterprise. Strategy most successful B2B SaaS converge on (GitLab, Sentry, Supabase). Defer Model B cost until a paying customer demands it.
nodes.norm_label: model c — hybrid (shared core + dedicated tier)

nodes.label: Model D — Source License / Self-Hosted
nodes.rationale: Niche but powerful in the GCC/regional market where banks and family offices demand on-prem. Lowest infra cost; highest support cost. Only worth pursuing once brand traction exists.

nodes.label: current_org_ids() SQL Helper Function
nodes.rationale: Define once as a SQL function so every RLS policy calls it — described as the single highest-leverage change in the whole project. Replaces per-policy auth.uid() = user_id checks.

nodes.label: Stripe Billing Integration (Checkout + Customer Portal + subscriptions table)
nodes.norm_label: stripe billing integration (checkout + customer portal + subscriptions table)

nodes.label: Transactional Email Provider (Resend/Postmark) per-tenant sender domains
nodes.rationale: Gmail App Passwords will throttle at scale with many tenants. Moving to Resend/Postmark with per-tenant sender domains improves deliverability and enables per-org branding of notification emails.
nodes.norm_label: transactional email provider (resend/postmark) per-tenant sender domains

nodes.label: Next.js 14 (App Router, Server Actions, Server Components)
nodes.norm_label: next.js 14 (app router, server actions, server components)

nodes.label: Supabase (PostgreSQL 15, GoTrue Auth, Row Level Security)
nodes.norm_label: supabase (postgresql 15, gotrue auth, row level security)

nodes.label: Investment Tracking Feature (ROI, commission, payout, duration types)
nodes.norm_label: investment tracking feature (roi, commission, payout, duration types)

nodes.label: Debt Management Feature (creditor/debtor, interest, partial payments)
nodes.norm_label: debt management feature (creditor/debtor, interest, partial payments)

nodes.label: Dual Language AR/EN with RTL Support
nodes.norm_label: dual language ar/en with rtl support

Cron Alert Delivery Pipeline: GitHub Action → API Endpoint → Notifications
